instruction: Help a customer who cannot reset their password.
input: The reset link says it has expired.
output: Ask the customer to request a new reset email, use only the newest link, and check that their device clock is accurate. If the replacement link also fails, escalate the account ID to identity support without requesting the password.

instruction: Respond to a customer reporting a duplicate card charge.
input: Two identical pending charges appeared after checkout.
output: Explain that one entry may be a temporary authorization, confirm the order was submitted once, and ask the customer to wait until the charges settle. Open a billing investigation if both charges post.

instruction: Handle a shipment that is three days late.
input: Tracking has not updated since the regional depot scan.
output: Acknowledge the delay, verify the delivery address and carrier status, then provide the revised delivery window. If there is no carrier movement within one business day, start a trace and offer the documented replacement options.

instruction: Guide a customer who wants to cancel a subscription.
input: They want cancellation at the end of the current billing period.
output: Confirm the renewal date and submit an end-of-period cancellation so access remains active through the paid term. State that no further renewal will be charged and provide the cancellation confirmation reference.

instruction: Answer a question about a missing refund.
input: The refund was approved six business days ago but is not visible.
output: Confirm the refund date, amount, and original payment method. Explain the normal bank processing window, provide the refund reference, and escalate to payments if that window has already passed.

instruction: Resolve a report that an item arrived damaged.
input: The outer package was crushed and the product screen is cracked.
output: Apologize, document the damage with the customer's consent, and offer the eligible replacement or refund path. Provide a prepaid return method when return shipment is required and avoid asking the customer to use the damaged device.

instruction: Respond to a request to change a shipping address.
input: The order was placed ten minutes ago and has not entered fulfillment.
output: Verify the order and the customer's authorization, then update the address if the fulfillment lock has not started. If edits are already locked, explain the carrier-intercept or cancel-and-reorder options without promising an intercept.

input: The customer does not recognize the device or location.
output: Treat the account as potentially compromised: invalidate active sessions, guide the customer through a password reset and multi-factor authentication, and review recent account changes. Do not request passwords or one-time codes.

instruction: Explain why a promotion code is not applying.
input: The code is valid this week but the cart contains a clearance item.
output: Check the promotion terms and explain the specific product exclusion in plain language. Suggest eligible items or another currently documented offer, but do not invent or manually promise an unauthorized discount.

instruction: Guide a customer requesting a copy of their account data.
input: They want their purchase history and profile information.
output: Direct the customer to the authenticated data-export workflow, explain the verification and delivery window, and note that sensitive exports are sent only through the approved secure channel.

instruction: Triage a mobile feature that closes unexpectedly.
input: The app closes when the customer opens saved items on version 8.4.
output: Capture the app version, operating system, reproducible steps, and whether the issue persists after a normal restart. Offer the safe cache or update steps in the support guide, then attach diagnostics to an engineering escalation if the crash repeats.

instruction: Help a customer change the application language.
input: They selected Spanish but part of the interface remains in English.
output: Confirm both the in-app language and device locale, ask the customer to restart the app after saving the preference, and record which screens remain untranslated if the mismatch persists so localization support can investigate.
